*Information about this image and its acquisition is forthcoming.*

M106 is an intermediate spiral galaxy with an active (Seyfert II) nucleus, in the constellation Canes Venatici. It lies approximately 23.5 million light-years away, shines at an apparent magnitude of 8.4, and spans roughly 18.6 by 7.2 arcminutes. Approximate coordinates: RA 12h 18m 58s, Dec +47° 18′.

*This description was generated by an AI assistant from general astronomical references, not from Jay's own notes on this image — treat it as a starting point, not a verified caption.*
